title: Public URL Shortening Page Implementation Steps

steps.title: Create frontend folder
details: Create the public-page folder.

steps.title: Create page file
details: Create public-page/index.html.

steps.title: Use standalone static frontend
details: Use plain HTML, CSS, and Vanilla JavaScript.
Do not use a frontend framework.
The file should be openable directly in a browser during development.

steps.title: Apply visual design
details: Use a dark background.
Use a minimal typographic style.
Use a single centered card layout.

steps.title: Add UI elements
details: Add page title: Paste your URL here.
Add one full-width text input.
Use placeholder: https://your-long-url.com/....
Add a Shorten button.
Add a result area hidden by default.
Show the generated short URL as a clickable link.
Add a Copy button.
Add an error message area for invalid URLs.

steps.title: Add API configuration
details: Define const API_BASE = "http://localhost:3000" at the top of the script block.
Use API_BASE for all fetch calls.

steps.title: Implement submit behavior
details: On Shorten button click, read the input value.
Also submit when the user presses Enter.
Check that the value starts with http:// or https://.
Show an inline error if validation fails.
POST to `${API_BASE}/shorten` with JSON body `{ originalUrl }`.
Send the Content-Type: application/json header.

steps.title: Handle API response
details: On success, show the returned shortUrl.
Display shortUrl as a clickable link.
On failure, show the API error message.

steps.title: Implement copy behavior
details: Use navigator.clipboard.writeText(shortUrl).
Change the Copy button label to Copied! after copying.

steps.title: Ensure backend dependencies exist
details: The backend must expose POST /shorten.
The createShortUrl controller must return shortUrl and shortCode.
cors() must be enabled in backend/server.js so the static page can call the API.
backendRequirements: createShortUrl controller response includes shortUrl and shortCode
